title: clock_gettime return value not checked in tx_queue.c
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/link/tx_queue.c
Line: 28-29

In tx_queue_posix_now_ms(), clock_gettime() return value is not checked:

```c
static uint32_t tx_queue_posix_now_ms(void)
{
    struct timespec ts;
    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts);  // No error check
    return (uint32_t)(ts.tv_sec * 1000 + ts.tv_nsec / 1000000);
}
```

clock_gettime() can fail with EINVAL (invalid clock_id) or EFAULT (invalid timespec pointer). On failure, ts is uninitialized and the returned timestamp would be garbage, leading to incorrect deadline calculations.

Impact: Packets could be expired prematurely or kept too long if time calculation uses uninitialized data.

Fix: Check return value and either fallback gracefully or abort.
